body:
### Problem

I'm on EndeavorOS Arch Linux x86-64, neovim version is:
```
NVIM v0.11.6
Build type: RelWithDebInfo
LuaJIT 2.1.1767980792
```
---
When I press `]d` or `[d` in the buffer where an LSP is active (tried with C++ file where clangd LSP is active and in Python file where pyright LSP is active), sometimes the right thing happens and the cursor jumps to the next diagnostic, but often (very often, like 1 in 2 times) I get an error message in the statusline area saying "E388: Couldn't find definition" when pressing `]d`, and "E349: No identifier under cursor" when pressing `[d`, even though both of those are mapped to `vim.diagnostic.jump{ count = 1, float = true }` in my neovim config. 
The output of `:nmap ]d` command is:
```
n  ]d          *@<Lua 202: ~/.config/nvim/init.lua:68>
n  ]d          * <Lua 29: vim/_defaults.lua:0>
                 Jump to the next diagnostic in the current buffer
```
and the output of `:nmap [d` command is:
```
n  [d          *@<Lua 203: ~/.config/nvim/init.lua:72>
n  [d          * <Lua 30: vim/_defaults.lua:0>
                 Jump to the previous diagnostic in the current buffer
```

Here's my full neovim config:
```lua
vim.o.shiftwidth = 2
vim.o.softtabstop = 2
vim.o.expandtab = true
vim.o.autoindent = true
vim.o.ignorecase = true
vim.o.smartcase = true
vim.o.wildignorecase = true
vim.o.incsearch = true
vim.o.scrolloff = 1
vim.o.scrolljump = -100
vim.o.guicursor = 'a:block'
vim.o.termguicolors = true
vim.o.timeout = true
vim.o.ttimeout = true
vim.o.timeoutlen = 60
vim.o.ttimeoutlen = 10

require 'paq' {
  'savq/paq-nvim',
  'neovim/nvim-lspconfig',
  {'nvim-treesitter/nvim-treesitter', build = ':TSUpdate'},
  'stevearc/oil.nvim',
  'windwp/nvim-autopairs',
  'f4z3r/gruvbox-material.nvim',
  'Mofiqul/vscode.nvim',
  'nickkadutskyi/jb.nvim',
}
require('oil').setup()

local treesitter = require('nvim-treesitter')
treesitter.setup{ install_dir = vim.fn.stdpath('data') .. '/treesitter' }
local treesitter_langs = {'c', 'cpp', 'cmake', 'lua', 'python', 'bash'}
treesitter.install(treesitter_langs)
vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('FileType', {
  pattern = vim.list_extend(treesitter_langs, {'sh'}),
  group = config_augroup,
  callback = function() vim.treesitter.start() end,
})
vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('FileType', {
  pattern = 'lua',
  group = config_augroup,
  command = 'setl omnifunc=',
})

local function should_disable_clangd()
  local env_var = os.getenv('DISABLE_CLANGD')
  if not env_var then return false end
  env_var = env_var:gsub("^%s*(.-)$s*$", "%1"):lower()
  return env_var == '1' or env_var == 'true' or env_var == 'yes' or
         env_var == 'y' or (tonumber(env_var) or 0) > 0
end

local lsp_lang_servers = {'clangd', 'pyright'}
vim.lsp.config('clangd', {
  root_dir = function(bufnr, on_dir)
    -- Logic to conditionally disable LSP client per buffer can be added here
    if should_disable_clangd() then return end
    on_dir(vim.fs.root(bufnr, vim.lsp.config.clangd.root_markers))
  end,
})
for _, lsp_serv_name in ipairs(lsp_lang_servers) do
  vim.lsp.config(lsp_serv_name, {
    on_attach = function(lsp_client, bufnr)
      vim.o.signcolumn = 'no'

      vim.keymap.set('n', ']d', function()
        vim.diagnostic.jump{ count = 1, float = true }
      end, {buffer = bufnr})

      vim.keymap.set('n', '[d', function()
        vim.diagnostic.jump{ count = -1, float = true }
      end, {buffer = bufnr})

      vim.keymap.set('n', '<f2>', function()
        vim.lsp.buf.rename()
      end, {buffer = bufnr})
    end,
  })
end
vim.lsp.enable(lsp_lang_servers)
```

I've tried adding `nowait = true` option to `vim.keymap.set()` for both of the `[d` and `]d` but to no avail. Calling the diagnostic function with `:lua vim.diagnostic.jump{ count = 1, float = true }` command works as expected with no issues.

### Steps to reproduce

Use my neovim config, then create a test C++ file `a.cc` with the following contents:
```c++
#include <stdio.h>

int main() {
  int a;
  a + 1;
  b - 0;
  return 0;
}
```
Ensure clangd is installed. Then open the file with `nvim a.cc` and then try pressing `]d` and `[d` few times, you'll notice that the right thing happens only sometimes, while most of the times an error is displayed in the statusline area.

### Expected behavior

Expected the `[d` and `]d` mappings to invoke the callback they are directly mapped to in the buffer, instead of doing it just _sometimes_ and falling back to some obscure default tag/definition functionality.

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

NVIM v0.11.6 Build type: RelWithDebInfo LuaJIT 2.1.1767980792

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

I don't use Vim.

### Operating system/version

EndeavorOS Arch Linux x86-64

### Terminal name/version

Alacritty

### $TERM environment variable

alacritty

### Installation

Arch Linux package manager -- pacman

comment_data.body:
> When I press `]d` or `[d` ... where an LSP is active ... sometimes the right thing happens ... but often I get an error message in the statusline area saying "E388: Couldn't find definition"

Works for me. 

Sorry, "use my config" is not a minimal repro. Not going to spend time debugging your config. If you can reduce the steps then we can reopen.

btw, are you using the `on_jump` callback? https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/c0f8b3fb668b734555c796e846ec764a132d2bff/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua#L1224-L1226

Yeah it's totally fine I understand. Anyway, I just added `vim.o.winborder = 'single'` and `vim.diagnostic.config{ virtual_text = true }` to my config and the bug has disappeared. Just throwing it out there, don't care enough to investigate further. Best of luck.

> > When I press `]d` or `[d` ... where an LSP is active ... sometimes the right thing happens ... but often I get an error message in the statusline area saying "E388: Couldn't find definition"
> 
> Works for me.
> 
> Sorry, "use my config" is not a minimal repro. Not going to spend time debugging your config. If you can reduce the steps then we can reopen.
> 
> btw, are you using the `on_jump` callback?
> 
> [neovim/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/c0f8b3fb668b734555c796e846ec764a132d2bff/runtime/lua/vim/diagnostic.lua#L1224-L1226)
> 
> Lines 1224 to 1226 in [c0f8b3f](/neovim/neovim/commit/c0f8b3fb668b734555c796e846ec764a132d2bff)
>  vim.schedule(function() 
>    opts.on_jump(diagnostic, api.nvim_win_get_buf(winid)) 
>  end)

By the way my config is like ~60 lines and is as simple as it can get. The fact that my issue is not considered "minimal reproduction" and the fact that you said "debug your config" really highlights just how bloated neovim is and the issues with the underlying architectural flaws of neovim and the very philosophy of the editir. OpenBSD vi (nvi) rocks, nvim is a bloat. I'm done with it. Feel sorry for you that you're a maintainer of this garbage bloat, bro :(
Get a life, seriously

Ignoring your temper tantrum: a minimal reproduction is focused and self-contained; countless examples on the tracker.  
In comparison, your script:

- Downloads _eight_ plugins, themselves containing _thousands_ of lines of additional code.
- Includes plugins like `gruvbox-material.nvim`, suggesting no real attempt to narrow down a cause.
- Requires pre-installed `paq.nvim` to bootstrap, so can't work with `nvim --clean` as instructed by the issue template.
  * [This repro template](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/contrib/minimal.lua) is even suggested for similar cases, but wasn't considered.

This is not reasonably minimal.

title: `]d` and `[d` result in "E388: Coudln't find definition" even though it's mapped to `vim.diagnostic.jump{ count = 1, float = true }`
